question_text: Let 𝑋ଵ, 𝑋ଶ, … , 𝑋௡ be a random sample from a population 𝑓(𝑥; 𝜃), where 𝜃 is a parameter. Then which one of the following statements is NOT true?

options.A: ௜ୀଵ is a complete and sufficient statistic for 𝜃, if 𝑓(𝑥; 𝜃) = 𝑒ିఏ𝜃௫ , 𝑥= 0, 1, 2, … , and 𝜃> 0
options.B: ௜ୀଵ , ∑ ௜ୀଵ ) is a complete and sufficient statistic for 𝜃, if 𝑓(𝑥; 𝜃) = √2𝜋𝜃 𝑒ି ଵ ଶఏమ(௫ିఏ)మ , −∞< 𝑥< ∞, 𝜃> 0
options.C: 𝑓(𝑥; 𝜃) = 𝜃𝑥ఏିଵ, 0 < 𝑥< 1, 𝜃> 0 has monotone likelihood ratio property in ௜ୀଵ
options.D: 𝑋(௡) −𝑋(ଵ) is ancillary statistic for 𝜃 if 𝑓(𝑥; 𝜃) = 1, 0 < 𝜃< 𝑥< 𝜃+ 1, where 𝑋(ଵ) = min{𝑋ଵ, 𝑋ଶ, … , 𝑋௡} and 𝑋(௡) = max{𝑋ଵ, 𝑋ଶ, … , 𝑋௡} Page 18
